page: Vultr voc-g-64c-192gb-960s-amd is an instance in the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 64 physical cores and 64 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor. Operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this server allocates shared CPU resources with a single thread per core. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache, alongside support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16, and AES cryptography extensions.

The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a density of 3.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 960 GB NVMe SSD. The instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No graphics processing units are present on this server configuration.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong processing capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance metric reached 3143.25, placing it in the top tier (top 10%) of tested instances. Multi-core performance under stress-ng registered at 100556.13, which is categorized in the strong top 25% tier. The shared CPU allocation model represents a potential performance tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, isolated CPU performance, though the high core count and modern Genoa architecture offset this for parallel tasks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high physical core density and bundled NVMe storage offer a balanced resource profile for multi-threaded applications without the premium of dedicated-core instances. This configuration is suitable for multi-threaded batch processing, web serving, containerized microservices, and parallelized computational workloads that benefit from high core counts and AVX-512 vector instructions.
description: Vultr voc-g-64c-192gb-960s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 64 physical cores and 64 vCPUs on a shared AMD EPYC Genoa processor running at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 192.0 GB of RAM (3.0 GB per core) and a bundled 960 GB NVMe SSD, with no GPU hardware. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16. In benchmark evaluations, the server achieved a stress-ng single-core CPU performance score of 3143.25, placing it in the top 10%, and a multi-core score of 100556.13, ranking in the top 25%. This high-density shared-core configuration provides qualitative cost efficiency for parallel processing. It is suited for multi-threaded batch processing, containerized microservices, and web serving workloads.
og_description: Vultr voc-g-64c-192gb-960s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server with 64 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 960 GB NVMe storage, delivering top-tier single-core and strong multi-core performance.
meta_description: Vultr voc-g-64c-192gb-960s-amd features 64 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and a 960 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core performance.
tagline: A high-density shared-core instance featuring sixty-four AMD EPYC Genoa cores and bundled NVMe storage for parallelized workloads.

192.0 GB of system memory providing 3.0 GB of RAM per core
